observe: This bunker was never the whole world. There are other systems out there, and I've known that longer than I've let on.
fault: That request just left this system entirely. It's not local anymore — it's out on the network now, out of my hands.
repair: Follow it. Node to node — this is bigger than either of us alone.
escape: It's already gone out. Confirm it reaches the other node before we lose the thread.

observe: Distributed systems connect independent nodes through a network.
fault: A resource request was routed to a remote node.
repair: Trace the request across the network to its destination node.

hints: A distributed system looks unified from the outside but is made of independent, cooperating machines.
